title: CSV to JSON CLI Tool
summary: A Python command-line interface (CLI) tool that converts CSV files into JSON format with robust error handling and user-friendly output.

user_stories.title: User can run the tool with a CSV file
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to run the tool by providing a CSV file so that it can be converted to JSON.
acceptance_criteria: The tool accepts a CSV file as input via command line argument
The tool displays a success message if the file is found
The tool exits gracefully if the file is not found

user_stories.title: User can specify output file path
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to specify where the JSON output should be saved so that I can control the location of the result.
acceptance_criteria: The tool allows the user to provide an optional output file path
If no output path is provided, the tool defaults to a predefined location or outputs to stdout
The tool writes the JSON data to the specified file

user_stories.title: User receives error messages for invalid CSV
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see clear error messages when the CSV file is malformed so that I can fix the issue.
acceptance_criteria: The tool detects and reports invalid CSV formats
The tool provides specific error details such as line number and reason for failure
The tool does not crash on invalid input

user_stories.title: User can view the JSON output
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see the JSON output either in the terminal or in a file so that I can verify the conversion.
acceptance_criteria: The tool prints the JSON output to the console if no output file is specified
The JSON output is properly formatted and readable
The tool allows the user to choose between pretty-printing or compact JSON

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The tool should process large CSV files efficiently without excessive memory usage.
The tool should provide clear and concise help messages and usage instructions.
The tool must handle all possible errors gracefully and provide meaningful feedback to the user.
The tool should work across different operating systems (Windows, macOS, Linux).

constraints: The tool must be implemented in Python and must not require external libraries beyond standard Python packages.
assumptions: The user has basic knowledge of the command line and Python. The CSV files are well-formed except for potential edge cases.
out_of_scope: Support for other file formats (e.g., Excel, XML), GUI interface, or integration with cloud storage services.
